title: The Big Cheese Ultra Power Live Multi-Catch Mouse Trap, Kills Mice, Weather-Proof Industrial Strength Galvanised Steel, Self-Setting, Traps Over 10 Mice, Large
features: GALVANISED STEEL: Long-lasting and hardwearing all-weather galvanised metal trap, Suitable for use in domestic and commercial sites; both outdoor and indoor including garages, garden sheds and outbuildings
SELF-SETTING: The trap has a self-setting mechanism for ease of use and will trap 10 or more live mice, For best results, bait with The Big Cheese Mouse and Rat Attractant (STV163, sold separately)
SECURE: The bait and catches are contained, making it safer where poison baits and snap traps may be a hazard, e,g,, in kitchens or around children and pets
MONITOR: Clear view flip-top lid for easy inspection, cleaning and live release
TESTED AND PROVEN: On-pack guarantee provided
POWERFUL: For big rodent problems, like the professionals use
description: From the manufacturer GUARANTEED EFFECTIVE RODENT CONTROL Bait & Kill: Our Ultra Power range offers the only true single-feed rodent poison available on the amateur market. The highly palatable formula contains a powerful active ingredient that delivers a lethal dose to rodents in just one feed. Catch & Kill: We offer a selection of versatile rodent trap solutions that are highly effective, with satisfaction guaranteed. Our powerful snap traps have been designed with durability in mind to be used again and again, whilst our innovative electronic products use smart-circuit technology to deliver a quick and humane kill. Pet-safe and child-safe options are available. Catch Alive: Safe for use around children and pets, these traps provide a humane, welfare-friendly alternative to lethal methods. Our professional-quality traps have been designed with safety in mind, both for the user and the target pest. Self-setting multi-catch mechanisms and fast-action closures allow for efficient rodent trapping and are ideal for large rodent infestations. Repel: 24/7 protection and peace of mind - our advanced mega-sonic repellers will protect your home around the clock, ridding your home of rodent pests and preventing their return. Poison-free and safe for use around children. Single trap RODENT CONTROL The Ultra Power Live Multi-Catch Mouse Trap from The Big Cheese provides effective control of mice in homes, farms and commercial premises without the use of poisons – JUST LIKE THE PROFESSIONALS USE. Professional-quality products you can trust. Experts in rodent control for over 25 years. Ultra Power Ultra Power Live-Catch Mouse Trap All-weather design trap Made from industrial strength galvanised steel to ensure years of reliable service. The clear inspection lid allows at-a-glance trap inspection and is hinged for easy cleaning and live release. The unique low profile of the trap enables it to be sited beneath counters and pallets, as well as within shallow ceiling voids and cavities. It is suitable for use in both indoor and outdoor areas and where poison baits and snap traps may be a hazard. eg. in kitchens or around children and pets. Traps 10 or more live mice in one setting. Clear view flip-top lid for easy inspection and cleaning. Self-setting mechanism. Indoor and outdoor use. Child and pet safe. Bait with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Rat & Mouse Attractant purchased separately. Part of the ULTRA POWER range. All-weather galvanised Engineered to deliver effective control of rodents. Live-Catch Mouse Traps from The Big Cheese are made from durable all-weather galvanised steel and have been trusted by generations of users suitable for use in any weather conditions. Live release Traps 10 or more live mice in one setting. ANIMAL WELFARE It is a legal requirement, under the Animal Welfare Act (2006), to protect trapped animals from unnecessary suffering. Traps must be checked at least once daily, and removed from use when this is not possible. Child and pet safe Safe to use around children and pets. The low profile trap allows you to place out of sight under cupboards etc but also keep captured rodents hidden from sight. Reusable To increase catch rate, bait traps with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse and Rat Attractant (purchase separately). Guaranteed effective rodent control Mice Mice are a highly adaptive and invasive species and they’re able to populate a variety of ecosystems. Agricultural fields, buildings, and boats are all viable habitats. In more temperate climates there will be even more possible nest sites. Swimming, climbing, jumping proficiency, and the two incisors on their upper jaw, all make for a formidable predator wherever they reside. However, despite all of these amazing abilities, the house mouse chooses to reside, as its name would indicate, in a home. House mice ( Mus Musculus Domesticus ) are a highly adaptive member of the genus Mus (mouse), yet for some reason they choose to live in the least demanding environment, a human home. They are attracted by the continuous flow of food and warmth, ideal for nest sites. The offer of food and shelter is so tempting that many choose to stay and breed year-round. Diet As one can imagine, the palate of a house mouse is highly adaptable since every habitat they migrate through will hold different opportunities for feeding. Outside dwellers will eat insects and a lot of grain, however, inside dwelling mice are practically omnivorous and, just like brown rats, will consume 10-15% of their body weight each day in just about anything we throw away. Most house mice will nibble on multiple different food sources to obtain their required nutrition, up to 20 times each night, since they’re nocturnal. A large proportion of a mouse’s 3g daily intake will be grain, namely cereal, favouring high-energy, man-made foods. Studies have proven that the levels of aggression in both rats and mice can be attributed to this high fat ‘human’ diet they have both been on for so long. These foods increase oestrogen levels and results in a shorter temper and longer periods of aggression. Evidence of intrusion 3-6 mm long droppings, black and rod-shaped. Approximately 80 per day, often appearing in clusters. Spotted in various places including attics, storage boxes, floorboards, disused cupboards. Check around fridges, ovens, heating units and washing machines too. Check wood, paper, cables, carpet and furniture for marks. Dark grey marks on surfaces from oil on hair. Although they don’t sweat, they emit a musky odour and urinate a lot, producing an ammonia smell. Hearing pattering feet in walls at night could be a sign of a mouse or rat infestation. If your dog is barking at night at what seems to be nothing, it could be picking up the scent or sounds of rodents.
